reforms.description: Slovenia adopted a capital market development strategy in 2023 aimed at improving access to equity finance for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), increasing the supply of financial products, and enhancing financial literacy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to equity finance for SMEs is expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially adopted and is in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the capital market to support SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The capital market development strategy adopted in 2023 rightly focuses on improving access of SMEs to equity finance.

reforms.description: The government plans to provide 0.5% of GDP in subsidies to the private sector for the deployment of public charging stations in remote areas and along motorways, complemented by EU support of 0.1% of GDP until the end of 2026.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Subsidies for charging stations are expected to enhance the adoption of electric vehicles and related technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant but narrower in scope, focusing on a specific infrastructure investment.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to provide 0.5% of GDP in subsidies to the private sector for the deployment of public charging stations in remote areas and along motorways.

reforms.description: The government announced a EUR 3.5 billion investment in electricity grids until 2032 to enhance the deployment of renewable energy and improve energy security.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investment in electricity grids is expected to facilitate renewable energy deployment, enhancing energy security and sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major investment in infrastructure critical for energy transition.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced EUR 3.5 billion (or 7.5% of GDP) in investment in electricity grids until 2032.

reforms.description: The government plans to abolish municipal restrictions on windmills to facilitate the deployment of renewable energy projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Removing restrictions on windmills is expected to promote renewable energy projects and innovation in energy technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant but focuses on regulatory changes rather than direct funding.
reforms.source_quote: To reduce the costs of the deployment of renewables, the government plans to abolish municipal restrictions on windmills.

reforms.description: The Slovenian government is amending the National Housing Act to improve conditions for the construction of social rental housing, aiming to attract private investments and enhance the sustainability of social housing projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Amending the National Housing Act is expected to attract private investments, enhancing the sustainability of social housing.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in housing policy to support social rental housing.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Solidarity-Based Future oversees reform efforts to amend the National Housing Act to improve framework conditions for the construction of social rental housing.
